description.en: The `UsePAM` directive enables the Pluggable Authentication Module (PAM) interface. If set to `yes` this will enable PAM authentication using `ChallengeResponseAuthentication` and `PasswordAuthentication` directives in addition to PAM account and session module processing for all authentication types.

rationale.en: When `usePAM` is set to `yes`, PAM runs through account and session types properly. This is important if you want to restrict access to services based off of IP, time or other factors of the account. Additionally, you can make sure users inherit certain environment variables on login or disallow access to the server

audit.en:
Edit the `/etc/ssh/sshd_config` file to set the `UsePAM` parameter to `yes` above any `Include` entries as follows:  

**Note:** First occurrence of an option takes precedence. If Include locations are enabled, used, and order of precedence is understood in your environment, the entry may be created in a file in Include location.

```
UsePAM yes
```

remediation.en:
Edit the `/etc/ssh/sshd_config` file to set the `UsePAM` parameter to `yes` above any `Include` entries as follows:  

**Note:** First occurrence of an option takes precedence. If Include locations are enabled, used, and order of precedence is understood in your environment, the entry may be created in a file in Include location.

```
UsePAM yes
```

references: NIST SP 800-53 Rev. 5: CM-1, CM-2, CM-6, CM-7, IA-5
